response.text: ```json { "description": "A versatile button component for user interaction and actions.", "semanticDescription": "The Button component provides a flexible and accessible way to trigger actions or navigate within an application. It supports various visual styles through its `variant` prop (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and different `size` options. Key features include a `loading` prop to indicate asynchronous operations and a powerful `asChild` prop, leveraging Radix Slot, to seamlessly render as a different HTML element (e.g., a link or custom component) while preserving button styling and functionality.", "tier": "free", "whenToUse": "Use the Button component to initiate actions, submit forms, trigger events, or navigate to different parts of the application. It's ideal for primary calls to action, confirming selections, or indicating an ongoing background process.", "whenNotToUse": "Avoid using a button for purely decorative purposes, as a simple text display, or for complex interactive elements that require more specific component logic (e.g., a checkbox, radio button, or a full dropdown menu). Do not use for navigation within the same page where a simple anchor tag (``) without button styling is more appropriate.", "patterns": [ "button", "async-action", "composition", "form-control" ], "tokens": [ "color-primary", "color-text", "spacing-sm", "spacing-md", "font-family-sans", "font-size-base", "border-radius-md" ], "examples": [ "", "", "", "" ], "relatedComponents": [ "Input", "Form", "Dialog", "Link", "Spinner" ], "a11yNotes": "Ensure buttons have a clear, descriptive text label. When `loading`, the button should be `disabled` to prevent multiple submissions. Use `aria-label` for icon-only buttons. The `asChild` prop retains accessibility benefits of the underlying element, but ensure the child element is semantically appropriate (e.g., an `` tag for navigation)." } ```
